spark.name: Spark
spark.description: A snapping bolt of static light, conjured from the air and flung at a foe. The first cantrip every delver learns — cheap, quick, and bright enough to sting. It strikes true so long as you can see your mark; only a creature's Ward blunts it.

regeneration.name: Regeneration
regeneration.description: A slow weave of mending light, drawn up by the caster's own will and sunk beneath the skin, where it knits torn flesh a little at a time. It cannot be hurled at a foe — only laid on the willing, be it your own hand or an ally's. Cast it on yourself, a fellow delver, or any creature you can see; the deeper your Intellect, the more it knits with each pulse.

chorus-of-mending.name: Chorus of Mending
chorus-of-mending.description: Where Regeneration is a single thread of mending light sunk into one body, the Chorus is that same weave sung wide — the caster raises a low, ringing note of glimmer that settles over everyone standing with them, delver and biddable creature alike, and knits their wounds a little at a time while it holds. It cannot be aimed at a foe, only spread over your own side, and its light is thinner spread across many than a single Regeneration laid on one — but in a hard fight with the party bloodied, a chorus mends the whole line at once where a single weave saves only one. The keener the caster's mind, the more each pulse knits.

glimmerskin.name: Glimmerskin
glimmerskin.description: A wardweave that draws a pinch of raw glimmer up out of the shards and crusts it over the skin in a thin, faintly luminous shell — part plate, part charm. It dulls a blade's bite and blunts hostile magic alike, but the glimmer burns away as it works, so the weave costs shards to cast. Lay it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; the keener your Intellect, the thicker the crust. Re-casting renews the shell rather than doubling it.

summon-wisp.name: Summon Wisp
summon-wisp.description: A weave that conjures a knot of cold light into a biddable shape and binds it to your will for a time. The conjured wisp drifts at your heel and spits its light at your foes, but the binding frays — the keener your Intellect the longer it holds, yet in time it always slips loose and the wisp unravels. Cast it and a Wisp answers; cast it again and the old one lets go as the new takes shape.

candlelight.name: Candlelight
candlelight.description: The humblest of human light-cantrips, taught to hedge-mages and night-travellers the world over: a delver kindles a mote of cold flame above the brow, just bright enough to push the dark back a single step. It sheds the barest light — and the steadier the caster's mind, the longer it holds before guttering out — but in a black drift it is the difference between blind and not. Cast it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; re-casting renews the mote rather than stacking it.

halo.name: Halo
halo.description: An Umbral glimmer-singer's weave, learned from the deep-folk who light their halls with cold flame and trust no torch: the caster cracks a lightbug's gland and draws its captive glow up into a steady ring of blue-white light that rides above the brow. It throws back the dark far better than a guttered candle, and the same cold glimmer that lights the way lies close against hostile magic and turns a measure of it aside — the keener the caster's mind, the brighter and longer it holds. The gland is spent in the kindling. Cast it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; re-casting renews the ring rather than stacking it.

mage-armour.name: Mage Armour
mage-armour.description: A wardweave of pure will rather than glimmer — the caster draws a lattice of hardened light close about a body, an invisible barding that turns a blade a fraction before it bites. It costs no shards, only concentration, and holds for a few minutes before it thins to nothing. The keener your Intellect, the tighter the weave. Lay it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see; re-casting renews the lattice rather than stacking it.

aegis.name: Aegis
aegis.description: Mage Armour drawn wide: rather than close a lattice of hardened light about one body, the caster throws it up over the whole line at once — a shared barding of will that settles on themselves and every ally standing with them, turning a blade a fraction before it bites. Like Mage Armour it is pure concentration, no glimmer, and holds a few minutes before it thins; the keener the caster's mind, the tighter each share of the weave. It cannot be raised over a foe, only your own side; re-casting renews the lattice rather than stacking it.

cleanse.name: Cleanse
cleanse.description: A weave of clean, scouring light drawn tight over the skin, meant not to mend a wound but to burn out what clings to one — witchfire, poison, any smouldering affliction eating away beneath the surface. It does nothing for a blow already struck, only for the slow harm still working. Cast it on yourself, an ally, or any creature you can see.

purge.name: Purge
purge.description: Where Cleanse is a scour of clean light drawn tight over one body, Purge is that same light loosed wide — the caster raises a hard, ringing pulse of it that washes the whole chamber and burns the clinging harm off everyone standing with them at once: witchfire, poison, any smouldering affliction the party carries. Like Cleanse it does nothing for a blow already struck, only for the slow harm still working — but where a single scour saves one, a Purge scours the whole line. It cannot touch a foe, only your own side.

sleep.name: Sleep
sleep.description: A hush of drowsy light poured into a foe's eyes, weighting its limbs until it folds where it stands and sinks into slumber. It deals no harm — its mercy is the opening it buys: a chance to slip away, or to set a killing blow before the sleeper stirs. Any blow rouses it at once, and a creature's Ward may shrug the weave off entirely. Cast it at a creature you can see.

bolt.name: Bolt
bolt.description: Where Spark merely stings, Bolt is a hurled lance of compressed light — slower to shape and hungrier for mana, but it lands with real weight and punches deep. The first true war-spell a delver learns, scaling hard with Intellect; only a creature's Ward turns it aside. Cast it at any foe you can see.

glimmer-spike.name: Glimmer Spike
glimmer-spike.description: The mage's heaviest single strike: a spike of raw glimmer dragged up out of the shards themselves and driven through a foe like a spear of frozen light. It burns mana and shards together, for the glimmer is spent in the casting and cannot be reclaimed — but nothing else a delver can throw bites as deep. It scales with Intellect, and Ward alone can blunt it. Cast it at any foe you can see.

messages.room: {caster} drives a spear of frozen light through {target}.

witchfire.name: Witchfire
witchfire.description: A weave that sinks a clinging, cold-burning glimmer into a foe's flesh — no great blow, but it smoulders and gnaws for seconds after, eating away even as you turn to the next mark. The burning glimmer sheds a faint light of its own, so a witchfired foe glows where it stands, marked in the dark. The mage's patient weapon: cast it and close, and let the burn do its work while your blade does yours. It clings a heartbeat for every point of your Intellect, so a keener mind burns longer and brighter; a creature's Ward shrugs the whole weave off, but once it catches nothing but death puts it out. Cast it at any foe you can see.

leech.name: Leech
leech.description: Hedge-craft turned to its oldest forbidden use: the caster hooks a thread of will into a living body and pulls, and what keeps it warm comes away down the line. No glimmer moves in this weave — it is human work, mana and nerve and a bad conscience — and what it takes the caster keeps, stolen warmth closing their own wounds as the mark's blood runs cold. A creature's Ward can shrug the hook off whole. Cast it at any foe you can see.

messages.self: You hook {spell} into {target} and pull — {damage} of their life comes away down the thread.
messages.room: {caster} hooks a pale thread into {target} and pulls.

summon-skeleton.name: Summon Skeleton
summon-skeleton.description: The Graveworker's graft of two crafts: human will to drive the weave, in the surface tradition — and raw glimmer spent outright, in the Umbral way, not woven as light but drawn out into hair-fine wire that strings a skeleton's joints and hauls it upright to serve. The risen thing is no husk and no ghost; there is nothing in it at all but the wire and your intent, and when the weave frays it drops back into a heap of honest bone. The keener your Intellect, the longer the binding holds. Cast it and a wired skeleton answers; cast it again and the old servant collapses as the new one rises.

arc-flash.name: Arc Flash
arc-flash.description: The delver gathers a charge of stored light to a snapping pitch and lets it arc loose all at once — a blinding crack of static brilliance that lashes every foe sharing the dark with you. Hungry for mana and scattered across a whole pack rather than driven deep into one, it is the war-spell for when the gloom comes at you many at once. It scales with Intellect, though a creature's Ward may yet earth the arc before it bites. Cast it and the room cracks white.

glimmer-storm.name: Glimmer Storm
glimmer-storm.description: Where Glimmer Spike drives all its glimmer into a single foe, the Storm scatters it wide — the caster drags a fistful of raw glimmer up out of the shards and flings it spinning through the whole chamber, a lashing squall of razor light that scores every foe sharing the dark. The glimmer is spent in the casting and cannot be reclaimed, so it burns shards as well as mana, and it asks a keen Intellect to shape so much light at once. Each foe's Ward may yet turn its share of the storm aside. Cast it and the room fills with cutting light.

flame-burst.name: Flame Burst
flame-burst.description: A delver's heaviest fire-work: a lump of bat-guano, rich with old saltpetre, dragged up and set off all at once in a single roaring bloom that catches every foe sharing the dark with you. What the initial blast doesn't finish keeps burning — a clinging fire gnaws at each survivor long after the flash fades, and the flame sheds its own light, so the burned mark themselves in the black. It asks a keen Intellect to shape so much fire at once, and burns mana to match; each foe's Ward may yet turn its share of the burst aside. Cast it and the room catches light.

iron-blast.name: Iron Blast
iron-blast.description: A weave that spends metal instead of glimmer: the caster drags a smelted iron bar up out of the pack, holds it a moment against the will, and bursts it apart into a whirling hail of shrapnel-hot iron that tears through every foe sharing the dark. It is a hurled blow, not a hex — no ward turns it, only iron and hide stand against it, so a foe's Armour blunts its bite where its Ward is useless. The bar is spent utterly in the shaping, and it asks a keen Intellect to hold so much iron together long enough to loose it. Cast it and the room fills with screaming metal.

iron-skin.name: Iron Skin
iron-skin.description: The defensive twin of Iron Blast, and the same trick turned inward: rather than burst the iron outward, the caster drags a smelted bar up out of the pack and works it thin over their own hide — a hammered shell of grey iron-light that clamps close and turns a blade where flesh would part. It is metal, not glimmer, so it does nothing against hostile magic — a warded bolt slides through it untouched — but against tooth and edge nothing a delver can weave holds harder. The bar is spent utterly in the shaping, and a keener mind beats the shell thinner and tighter. It can only be worked over your own skin, never laid on another; re-casting renews the shell rather than doubling it.

glimmer-husk.name: Glimmer Husk
glimmer-husk.description: An Umbral working, learned from the deep-folk who build rather than conjure: the caster sets down a worked chitin plate, drags raw glimmer up out of the shards, and binds it through the shell until the dead plate shudders and stands. What rises is a slow, spined husk — no great striker, but armoured and patient, and it plants itself between its maker and the dark to take the blows a mage cannot. The glimmer is spent in the binding and the plate is worked past saving, so the weave costs both; the keener your Intellect, the longer the binding holds before it frays. Bind another and the first slumps lifeless.

drink-light.name: Drink the Light
drink-light.description: Not a weave of light but its unmaking. The dark gathers itself, and the glow is drawn out of the air — torch, lamp, and kindled cantrip all guttering at once — until the chamber drowns in black. It is the working of things that hate the light and cannot bear it on their skin: drink the room dry, and the dark is theirs again. A creature reads the world by other senses than sight, and hunts the blind it leaves behind.

snuff.name: Snuff
snuff.description: A grasping reach of dark closes over a delver's flame and pinches it dead — torch, lantern, or kindled spark, it makes no difference to the thing that hates them all. The light is simply gone, and must be struck anew in a dark that was only ever waiting for it to fail.

gloom-bolt.name: Gloom Bolt
gloom-bolt.description: Not a weave a delver could learn, but a thing the deep does to what lingers too long against it: the crawler gathers the cold dark to its blind front and flings it like spat venom, a clot of hungry black that bites where it lands and seems, for a heartbeat, to drink the warmth it tears loose. It is the Starving Dark's appetite gone small and portable, carried in a beast that was never meant to hold it. Only Ward turns it aside.

leech-warmth.name: Leech Warmth
leech-warmth.description: Not a blow but a thirst. The void leech settles against a delver and drinks — not blood, not heat, but the warmth of will itself, the same spark a mage burns to shape a weave. One leech takes little; a swarm of them, clinging in the dark, can wring a caster dry before a flame can be struck. It leaves no wound, only the cold, hollow ache of a will spent that was never spent, and a spellbook gone useless in the black.
